controls.framework_name_en: OECD Framework for the Classification of AI Systems
controls.framework_name_ar: إطار منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية لتصنيف أنظمة الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Medium-action autonomy (human-on-the-loop)
controls.control_text_full: Medium-action autonomy (also referred to as "human-on-the-loop"): System evaluates input and acts upon its recommendations or output unless the human vetoes.
controls.intent_summary: In medium-action autonomy systems, the AI executes actions automatically unless a human actively intervenes to stop it — requiring robust monitoring and easy veto mechanisms.
evidence_signals: System logs show human veto events and reasons
UI includes a prominent, one-click veto button for operators
Alerts trigger when system actions exceed predefined thresholds
System documentation specifies default action behavior and override protocol
Operator training includes emergency veto procedures
evidence_anti_signals: No visible or accessible veto mechanism for operators
Veto events are not logged or tracked
System operates without monitoring or alerting for potential errors
controls.remediation_template: For medium-action autonomy systems, implement a clear, accessible, and logged human veto mechanism that allows operators to immediately halt any AI action — and train users on its critical importance.
controls.framework_name_en: OECD Framework for the Classification of AI Systems
controls.framework_name_ar: إطار منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية لتصنيف أنظمة الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: High-action autonomy (human-out-of-the-loop)
controls.control_text_full: High-action autonomy (also referred to as "human-out-of-the-loop"): System evaluates input and acts upon its recommendations or output without human involvement.
controls.intent_summary: High-action autonomy systems operate entirely without human intervention — requiring the highest level of safety validation, accountability, and ethical review due to their potential for uncontrolled impact.
evidence_signals: Independent safety certification for fully autonomous operation
Continuous monitoring and logging of all autonomous decisions
Ethics review board approval for deployment in high-risk contexts
System designed with multiple layers of fail-safes and redundancy
Regulatory compliance documentation for unattended operation
evidence_anti_signals: No safety or ethics review documented for autonomous deployment
System operates without monitoring, logging, or rollback capability
Deployment in safety-critical environments without regulatory approval
controls.remediation_template: If deploying a high-action autonomy system, you must obtain independent safety and ethics certification, implement comprehensive monitoring and fail-safes, and secure regulatory approval — especially in contexts affecting human life or rights.
controls.framework_name_en: OECD Framework for the Classification of AI Systems
controls.framework_name_ar: إطار منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية لتصنيف أنظمة الذكاء الاصطناعي

controls.control_title: Use of agreed standards for AI system evaluation
controls.control_text_full: In some cases, there are agreed standards or general methods to assess an AI system or application within a given industry context and for a type of task(s):  There are industry standards for evaluating AI systems as applied to this specific task and context.
controls.intent_summary: When industry standards exist for evaluating AI systems, organizations must use them to ensure consistent, reliable, and accountable assessment of system performance and safety.
evidence_signals: Reference to ISO, NIST, IEC, or other recognized standards in evaluation plan
Test results aligned with standardized benchmarks (e.g., F1-score, ROUGE, fairness metrics)
Third-party validation using industry-accepted evaluation protocols
Documentation showing compliance with sector-specific AI evaluation guidelines
Audit reports referencing standardized evaluation criteria
evidence_anti_signals: Evaluation methods are proprietary or undefined
Claims of 'high accuracy' without reference to any standard metric
Use of custom metrics not validated or peer-reviewed
controls.remediation_template: When industry standards exist for evaluating your AI system’s task, you must adopt and document their use in your evaluation plan — and avoid relying on unvalidated or opaque metrics.
controls.framework_name_en: OECD Framework for the Classification of AI Systems
controls.framework_name_ar: إطار منظمة التعاون الاقتصادي والتنمية لتصنيف أنظمة الذكاء الاصطناعي
